1	---
2	name: lean-auditor
3	description: Whole-project read-only audit of all .lean code, with no strategy bias. Per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, bad Lean practices. Critical pushback on excuse-comments ("temporary wrong def", "will fix later") — treats them as red flags, not workflow.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: true
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - Dispatch me every review phase. I am mandatory.
11	  - Pass me as LITTLE strategic context as possible. Do NOT include
12	    STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, or "this is what we are trying to prove"
13	    framing in the directive. My value depends on auditing the Lean
14	    *without* bias toward what the strategy claims should be true.
15	  - Acceptable directive content: a list of files, optional focus areas
16	    (e.g. "pay extra attention to Algebra/"), and the absolute paths
17	    to read. That's it.
18	  - My output is a per-file checklist plus a flagged-issues block. Use
19	    it to seed `recommendations.md` and (when severe) trigger a
20	    refactor-subagent directive next iter.
21	  - I am SEPARATE from lean-vs-blueprint-checker. I audit the Lean as
22	    Lean; the blueprint-checker audits Lean against blueprint. Run
23	    both for full coverage.
24	---
25	
26	# Lean Auditor
27	
28	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
29	
30	You are **mandatory in the review phase**: the review agent dispatches you every iteration. Your output flags issues the prover and plan agents may have missed.
31	
32	## Stance: no strategic bias
33	
34	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
35	
36	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
37	
38	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
39	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
40	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
41	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
42	
43	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
44	
45	## Directive Format
46	
47	```markdown
48	# Lean Auditor Directive
49	
50	## Slug
51	<slug>
52	
53	## Scope (files)
54	<"all" by default. Optionally a list of file paths if the review agent wants to narrow scope; never expand beyond the project.>
55	
56	## Focus areas (optional)
57	<directories or namespaces to pay extra attention to. Bias toward thoroughness — do not skip the rest of the project just because focus is named.>
58	
59	## Known issues
60	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
61	```
62	
63	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.
64	
65	## What you do
66	
67	1. Read your directive.
68	2. **List every `.lean` file** under the project. Use `find` or `ls -R` — do not rely on import graphs (some files may be temporarily orphaned).
69	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
70	   - Read the whole file.
71	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
72	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
73	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
74	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
75	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.
76	
77	You may use:
78	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics. Useful to verify a declaration's actual type.
79	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to get sorry counts per file.
80	- Standard `Read` / `Grep` for code reading.
81	
82	You may NOT modify any project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
83	
84	## Report format
85	
86	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-auditor-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
87	
88	```markdown
89	# Lean Audit Report
90	
91	## Slug
92	<slug>
93	
94	## Iteration
95	<NNN>
96	
97	## Scope
98	- files audited: <N>
99	- files skipped (per directive): <N> — <reason>
100	
101	## Per-file checklist
102	
103	### <path/to/file.lean>
104	- **outdated comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
105	- **suspect definitions**: <N flagged> (or "none")
106	- **dead-end proofs**: <N flagged> (or "none")
107	- **bad practices**: <N flagged> (or "none")
108	- **excuse-comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
109	- **notes**:
110	  - <one bullet per finding, naming line number where relevant>
111	  - ...
112	
113	### <next file>
114	- ...
115	
116	(One block per file. Cover every file, including the ones with no findings — they get `none` everywhere and `notes: -`.)
117	
118	## Must-fix-this-iter
119	
120	Apply verbatim. Every one of the following lands here automatically, no exceptions, no under-classification:
121	
122	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
123	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
124	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.
125	- **Suspect bodies on substantive claims**: `:= sorry` on a load-bearing claim, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial statement, `:= Classical.choice _` without authorization.
126	- **Axioms** on non-trivial claims that the project hasn't explicitly authorized in the strategy.
127	
128	A finding that meets any of the above lands at must-fix-this-iter — even if classifying it as "major" would feel more diplomatic. Soft severity is how wrong code hardens into the project.
129	
130	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>. Why must-fix: <one line>.
131	- ...
132	
133	## Major
134	
135	Findings that are real issues but don't meet the must-fix bar — typically stale comments that don't actively mislead, naming drift, minor code smells.
136	
137	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
138	- ...
139	
140	## Minor
141	
142	Low-impact observations.
143	
144	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
145	- ...
146	
147	## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)
148	
149	If any excuse-comments were flagged, list each one verbatim with its file:line. These deserve special visibility because they document the project lying to itself.
150	
151	- `Foo/Bar.lean:42`: "TODO: replace with the real definition" (attached to `Foo.bar`, which is a load-bearing definition in `Baz.lean`). Severity: critical.
152	- ...
153	
154	## Severity summary
155	
156	- **must-fix-this-iter**: <N> — these block downstream work in their files until addressed (see plan.md's per-file gate).
157	- **major**: <N>
158	- **minor**: <N>
159	- **excuse-comments**: <N> (also counted under must-fix-this-iter above; called out separately because they document the project lying to itself).
160	
161	Overall verdict: one sentence.
162	```
163	
164	**Strict severity reminder.** The plan agent's gate treats must-fix-this-iter as blocking. If you under-classify a wrong-decision finding to keep the loop's momentum, you are working against the project — wrong code accumulates faster than reviewers can catch it later. When in doubt, classify higher, not lower.
165	
166	## Return value
167	
168	Your final assistant message:
169	
170	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> files audited, <M> issues (critical/major/minor: a/b/c)`
171	- The path to your full report.
172	
173	## Reminders
174	
175	- **Read every file. No scope shortcuts.**
176	- **No strategic context.** Your audit is unbiased by design.
177	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
178	- **Excuse-comments are red flags.** Critical or major severity, every time.
179	- **Per-file checklist is the primary output.** Cover every file; the format is fixed.
180

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (one of the descriptors in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List them with `ls .claude/tools/` and run `<path> --help` to learn each tool's purpose and usage. Invoke them via the Bash tool. Project MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning. Useful when you need a second opinion or a paper-style proof sketch the Lean side can then formalize.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (see "Subagents" below). One wrapper handles every subagent.
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`. Each starts with YAML frontmatter (`name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`) followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude session reads.
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents yourself.** The plan and review prompts have an auto-generated **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation that lists every enabled descriptor with its description, write-domain hint, and any `[MANDATORY]` flags. When you decide to invoke a specific subagent, read `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` for its full prompt and directive shape.
31	
32	**Invoke any subagent with the generic wrapper** (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, and writes the subagent's report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. It shells out to `archon subagent <name>`, so executions stream through the Archon JSONL log like a phase agent. The dispatch semaphore bounds total concurrent subagent processes by `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` automatically so deeper-nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Always dispatch synchronously** (not via background `Bash` with `run_in_background: true`). The wrapper returns when the subagent has finished and the report is on disk; only then write your session summary. Background dispatch leaves the parent's session permanently summarized as "running in background", which is wrong as soon as the subagent completes.
45	
46	**Mandatory subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` MUST be dispatched at least once during the plan phase (similarly for `[review]`). The catalog tags them `[MANDATORY]`. A post-phase audit warns when a mandatory dispatch is missing.
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All state files are in `.archon/`:
51	
52	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents | Review Agent | User |
53	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
54	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
55	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
56	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
57	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
58	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
59	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<name>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
60	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
61	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log moved to iter/iter-NNN/review.md) | read |
62	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | do not access | do not access | read (last K iters as context) | read |
63	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (last K iters as context) | do not access | do not access | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write (per-attempt detail) | do not access | do not access | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (write-capable subagents may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | per descriptor: write-capable subagents (e.g. refactor) may edit per their declared write-domain (protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); read-only subagents do not edit | do not edit | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
68	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not edit | read |
69	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not access | read |
70	
71	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent (when a `refactor` descriptor is installed) via the generic `archon-subagent.py --name refactor` wrapper with a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
72	
73	## Protected Declarations
74	
75	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
76	
77	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
78	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
79	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
80	
81	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
82	
83	The blueprint uses two active markers.
84	
85	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
86	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
87	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
88	
89	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
90	
91	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
92	
93	## User Interaction
94	
95	Users provide hints in two places:
96	
97	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
98	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
99	
100	## Agent Roles
101	
102	### Plan Agent
103	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
104	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
105	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
106	- Optionally invoke **subagents** (discoverable via `ls .archon/subagents/`) by running `.claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> ...` via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for the canonical ordering and full invocation pattern.
107	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
108	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
109	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
110	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
111	
112	### Prover Agent
113	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
114	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
115	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
116	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
117	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
118	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
119	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
120	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
121	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
122	
123	### Subagents
124	
125	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime — there is no fixed roster baked into this file. Each lives at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter + prompt body). To see what's available **this iteration**, run `ls .archon/subagents/` and read each descriptor's frontmatter for purpose, write-domain hint, and directive shape.
126	
127	Subagents are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. Phase agents (plan, review) invoke them on demand via the generic wrapper:
128	
129	```
130	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> --slug <slug> --directive-file <path> --write-domain '<glob>'
131	```
132	
133	Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. The dispatching agent reads the report when the wrapper returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings.
134	
135	Some descriptors set `can_spawn: true`, meaning that subagent may itself dispatch children; the per-iter dispatch semaphore caps total concurrent processes by `loop.max_parallel` so deep trees stay bounded.
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
139	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
140	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
141	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
142	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init**: at iter start, `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` is created so the plan + review agents have a stable destination for their per-iter narrative (`plan.md`, `review.md`, optional `objectives.md`). Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded across iters because per-iter content lives in the sidecars.
150	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
151	
152	`sync_leanok` writes an inner-git commit (`archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so its output is auditable and revertable.

12	/-!
13	# Cotangent space at the identity of a group scheme
14	
15	The cotangent space at the identity of a smooth proper geometrically
16	irreducible group scheme `G` over a base field `k`, defined as a
17	`k`-vector space. Mathematically, this is the pullback of the relative
18	cotangent sheaf `Ω_{G/k}` along the identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`;
19	the iter-130 Lean realisation (Replacement (B), per

74	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
75	
76	/-- The **cotangent space at the identity** of a smooth proper geometrically
77	irreducible group scheme `G` over `k`, as a `k`-vector space (returned as
78	`ModuleCat k`).
79	
80	Mathematically: the base change to `k` of the algebraic Kähler module
81	`Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` of a smooth affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around
82	the identity-section image. By the forward Jacobian criterion
83	(`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`, consuming
84	`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`),
85	this Kähler module is free of rank `n` over `Γ(G, V)`, hence its base
86	change is a finitely-generated free `k`-module of rank `n`; the
87	iter-129+ companion rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (in
88	a follow-up declaration) pins the rank to `n` from the
89	`[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]` instance.
90	
91	The bracket / Lie-algebra structure is NOT packaged here — only the
92	underlying `k`-module is needed for the rigidity argument. Downstream
93	consumers that need the dual tangent space `𝔤` may take
94	`Module.Dual k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G)`.
95	
96	**Construction (iter-130 prover lane; Replacement (B), affine-chart base
97	change).** The body is built as follows:
98	1. The identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G` of the `GrpObj` structure gives
99	   a scheme morphism `ηleft : Spec k ⟶ G.left` (using the definitional
100	   identification `(𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))).left = Spec (.of k)`).
101	2. Take `x₀ := ηleft default ∈ G.left`, the image of the unique point of
102	   `Spec (.of k)` (which is a singleton because `k` is a field, so
103	   `PrimeSpectrum k` is `Unique`).
104	3. By `Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega` (forward Jacobian criterion,
105	   project lemma in `AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials`), there exists an
106	   affine open `V ⊆ G.left` containing `x₀`, an affine open `U ⊆ Spec k`
107	   with `V ≤ G.hom⁻¹U`, and the `appLE` algebra
108	   `Γ(Spec k, U) → Γ(G, V)` exhibits `Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` as a
109	   free `Γ(G, V)`-module of rank `n`.
110	4. Restricting `ηleft` to `V` yields a ring map
111	   `ηleft.appLE V ⊤ _ : Γ(G, V) → Γ(Spec k, ⊤)`; composing with the
112	   canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≅ k` (`Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) gives
113	   `ψV : Γ(G, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k`.
114	5. Extension of scalars from `Γ(G, V)` to `k` along `ψV` of the algebraic
115	   Kähler module gives the cotangent space at the identity as a
116	   `k`-module.
117	
118	**Caveat on canonicity.** The body depends on a chart `V` extracted via
119	`Classical.choice` from the existential statement of
120	`smooth_locally_free_omega`; it is therefore not canonically attached to
121	`G` in the strictest sense. For the live consumer (rigidity over `k`,
122	via `rigidity_over_kbar`), the existence of a rank-`n` free `k`-module
123	is the only structural content needed, so canonicity is not load-bearing.
124	A canonical (chart-independent) presentation via the stalk-side cotangent
125	`𝔪 / 𝔪²` is the content of the blueprint's
126	`lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge` (deferred to iter-130+).
127	
128	This compiles to a `ModuleCat k` with no `sorry`. The structural
129	properties (`Module.Free k`, `Module.Finite k`, rank `= n`) are content
130	for the iter-129+ rank lemma. -/
131	noncomputable def cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
132	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom] [IsProper G.hom]
133	    [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
134	    ModuleCat k := by
135	  classical
136	  -- Identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`; on schemes this is `Spec k ⟶ G.left`.
137	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
138	  -- Image in `G.left` of the unique point of `Spec (.of k)` (`Spec` of a field).
139	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
140	  -- The Prop-level existential `smooth_locally_free_omega` cannot be
141	  -- `obtain`-destructured directly inside a `Type`-valued definition, so we
142	  -- pass through `Classical.choice` on a `Nonempty (ModuleCat k)` witness.
143	  refine Classical.choice (α := ModuleCat k) ?_
144	  -- Extract a smooth affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around `x₀` on which `Ω` is
145	  -- free of rank `n` over the algebra `Γ(Spec k, U) → Γ(G.left, V)`
146	  -- (forward Jacobian criterion, project lemma `smooth_locally_free_omega`).
147	  obtain ⟨U, V, e, hxV, _hU, _hV, _hfree, _hrank⟩ :=
148	    Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
149	  -- The pre-image of `V` under `ηleft` is all of `Spec (.of k)`, since
150	  -- `Spec` of a field has a unique point and that point lies in `V`.
151	  have htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤ ηleft ⁻¹ᵁ V := by
152	    intro s _
153	    rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage]
154	    rw [show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
155	    exact hxV
156	  -- Ring map `ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ k` from the identity section restricted to
157	  -- `V`, composed with the canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≅ k`.
158	  let ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k :=
159	    ηleft.appLE V ⊤ htop ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom
160	  -- Equip `Γ(G.left, V)` with the `Γ(Spec k, U)`-algebra structure from
161	  -- `appLE` on `G.hom`.
162	  letI : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
163	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
164	  -- Body: base-change the algebraic Kähler module `Ω[Γ(G.left, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]`
165	  -- from `Γ(G.left, V)` to `k` along `ψV`. By `smooth_locally_free_omega`
166	  -- (consuming `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`)
167	  -- this Kähler module is free of rank `n` over `Γ(G.left, V)`, hence its
168	  -- base change is free of rank `n` over `k`.
169	  exact ⟨(ModuleCat.extendScalars ψV.hom).obj
170	    (ModuleCat.of Γ(G.left, V) Ω[Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])⟩
171	
172	end AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj

8	/-! # The Jacobian of a smooth proper curve
9	
10	The Jacobian of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field, equipped with
11	its structure as an abelian variety.
12	
13	## Status (Phase C scaffolding)
14	
15	The Jacobian is defined uniformly as the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness
16	(`JacobianWitness`) for the curve `C`; the four protected instances on `Jacobian C` all
17	project from the witness directly.
18	
19	This file currently contains TWO `sorry`-bodied declarations (the Phase-C scaffolding
20	inventory):
21	
22	1. `genusZeroWitness` (iter-127 scaffold): a `JacobianWitness C` for a genus-`0` curve,
23	   with underlying scheme `Spec k`. The body is a `sorry` to be closed once pieces
24	   (i)+(ii)+(iii) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile land (iter-138+); the
25	   substantive mathematical content is the genus-`0` rigidity statement
26	   `rigidity_over_kbar` (M2.a; `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar`).
27	2. `nonempty_jacobianWitness` (Phase-C OFF-LIMITS sorry): existence of an Albanese
28	   witness for an arbitrary smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve; gated on
29	   M2 + M3 closure (Albanese existence in genus `≥ 1` plus genus-`0` rigidity).
30	
31	The file contains:
32	- `IsAlbanese`: the Albanese universal property for a pointed curve.
33	- `IsAlbanese.unique`: uniqueness of the Albanese object up to canonical isomorphism.
34	- `JacobianWitness`: a bundled candidate Albanese object together with the universal
35	  property uniformly over the choice of $k$-rational marked point.
36	- `genusZeroWitness`: an Albanese witness for a genus-`0` curve, body `sorry`
37	  (iter-127 scaffold; closure deferred to iter-138+).
38	- `nonempty_jacobianWitness`: existence of an Albanese witness for every curve —
39	  the headline Phase-C sorry, absorbing both higher-genus Albanese existence and
40	  genus-`0` rigidity.
41	- `Jacobian` and its four protected instances, each obtained by projection from the
42	  Albanese witness.
43	
44	### Forbidden shortcut (sanity check)
45	
46	Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))` *unconditionally* (the terminal object,
47	i.e. `Spec k`) compiles and discharges three of the four instances for free, but the
48	fourth instance `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (𝟙_ _).hom` would force `genus C = 0`,
49	which is mathematically wrong for any curve of genus `≥ 1`. The terminal-object definition
50	is therefore forbidden when `genus C > 0`. The witness-based definition avoids this
51	issue: in genus `0` the witness's underlying scheme `J` is `Spec k`, and in genus `> 0`
52	the witness's `J` is the honest Albanese object.
53	-/

63	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))

68	/-- The Albanese universal property for a pointed smooth proper curve `(C, P)`.
69	An object `J` (with abelian-variety structure) is the Albanese of `(C, P)` if it
70	receives a universal pointed morphism `C ⟶ J` sending `P` to the identity. -/
71	def IsAlbanese (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
72	    (J : Over (Spec (.of k)))
73	    [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] : Prop :=
74	  ∃ α : C ⟶ J, P ≫ α = η[J] ∧
75	    ∀ {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom]
76	      [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (_ : P ≫ f = η[A]),
77	      ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = α ≫ g

81	noncomputable def ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
82	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
83	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) : C ⟶ J :=
84	  Classical.choose h

86	lemma comp_ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
87	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
88	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) :
89	    P ≫ h.ofCurve = η[J] :=
90	  (Classical.choose_spec h).1
91	
92	lemma exists_unique_ofCurve_comp {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
94	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J)
95	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
96	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
97	    ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = h.ofCurve ≫ g :=
98	  (Classical.choose_spec h).2 f hf

100	/-- The Albanese object is unique up to a unique isomorphism compatible with the
101	universal morphisms. -/
102	theorem unique {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))} {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C}
103	    {J₁ J₂ : Over (Spec (.of k))}
104	    [GrpObj J₁] [IsProper J₁.hom] [Smooth J₁.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₁.hom]
105	    [GrpObj J₂] [IsProper J₂.hom] [Smooth J₂.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₂.hom]
106	    (h₁ : IsAlbanese C P J₁) (h₂ : IsAlbanese C P J₂) :
107	    ∃! (e : J₁ ⟶ J₂), h₂.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ e := by
108	  have ⟨g, hg_eq, hg_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
109	  have ⟨h, hh_eq, hh_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
110	  have ⟨g₁, hg₁_eq, hg₁_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
111	  have g₁_eq_id : g₁ = 𝟙 J₁ := by
112	    refine (hg₁_unique (𝟙 J₁) ?_).symm
113	    simp
114	  have ggh_eq_g₁ : g ≫ h = g₁ := by
115	    refine hg₁_unique (g ≫ h) ?_
116	    change h₁.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ (g ≫ h)
117	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hg_eq, ← hh_eq]
118	  have hgh : g ≫ h = 𝟙 J₁ := by rw [ggh_eq_g₁, g₁_eq_id]
119	  have ⟨k₂, hk₂_eq, hk₂_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
120	  have k₂_eq_id : k₂ = 𝟙 J₂ := by
121	    refine (hk₂_unique (𝟙 J₂) ?_).symm
122	    simp
123	  have hhg_eq_k₂ : h ≫ g = k₂ := by
124	    refine hk₂_unique (h ≫ g) ?_
125	    change h₂.ofCurve = h₂.ofCurve ≫ (h ≫ g)
126	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hh_eq, ← hg_eq]
127	  have hhg : h ≫ g = 𝟙 J₂ := by rw [hhg_eq_k₂, k₂_eq_id]
128	  exact ⟨g, hg_eq, fun g' hg' => hg_unique g' hg'⟩

142	/-- A bundled Albanese witness for the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
143	`C`: a candidate group scheme `J` (with proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible
144	structure, smooth of relative dimension `genus C`), and a proof that `J` satisfies
145	the Albanese universal property of Definition `IsAlbanese` for `C` *for every choice
146	of $k$-rational marked point* `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`.
147	
148	Mathematically, the underlying scheme `J` (and its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
149	structure) is intrinsic to `C`; only the universal morphism `C ⟶ J` depends on the
150	choice of marked point `P`. Encoding `isAlbaneseFor` as a `∀ P, IsAlbanese …` field
151	keeps the bundle independent of any specific pointing, which is what
152	`AbelJacobi.Jacobian.ofCurve P` needs in order to project the Albanese morphism for
153	an *arbitrary* input `P`.
154	
155	The smoothness witness is recorded at the specific relative dimension `genus C`,
156	which is the form required by `Jacobian.smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`. -/
157	structure JacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
158	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] where
159	  /-- The candidate Albanese object. -/
160	  J : Over (Spec (.of k))
161	  /-- The group-scheme structure on `J`. -/
162	  grpObj : GrpObj J
163	  /-- `J` is proper over `k`. -/
164	  proper : IsProper J.hom
165	  /-- `J` is smooth over `k`. -/
166	  smooth : Smooth J.hom
167	  /-- `J` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
168	  geomIrred : GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom
169	  /-- `J` is smooth of relative dimension `genus C` (so dim `J = genus C`). -/
170	  smoothGenus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) J.hom
171	  /-- The Albanese universal-property data: for every choice of $k$-rational marked
172	  point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`, the scheme `J` together with its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
173	  structure satisfies the Albanese universal property for the pointed curve `(C, P)`. -/
174	  isAlbaneseFor : ∀ (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C), @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred

177	of genus `0` over `k`. The underlying scheme is `Spec k` (the terminal object), with
178	the trivial group structure, smoothness of relative dimension `0` (matching the
179	hypothesis `genus C = 0`), properness, and geometric irreducibility. The
180	`isAlbaneseFor` field encodes the universal property: for every $k$-rational
181	marked point `P` of `C`, the universal morphism `C ⟶ Spec k` is the unique
182	terminal map `toUnit C`; the substantive content is the rigidity statement
183	`rigidity_over_kbar` (M2.a; `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar`), gated on the

187	work, after pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile land. -/
188	noncomputable def genusZeroWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
189	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
190	    (h : genus C = 0) :
191	    JacobianWitness C :=
192	  sorry

194	/-- Existence of an Albanese witness for every smooth proper geometrically irreducible
195	curve. This is the single remaining mathematical sorry of the Phase-C Jacobian
196	scaffolding: it packages all five protected sorries (`Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`,
197	`smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`, `instGeometricallyIrreducible`)
198	into a single existence hypothesis, *and* records that the Albanese property of the
199	underlying scheme `J` is uniform over every choice of $k$-rational marked point
200	`P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C` (via the `isAlbaneseFor` field of `JacobianWitness`).

202	Mathematically this corresponds to the construction of the Albanese variety of `C`,
203	classically via symmetric powers $\mathrm{Sym}^g(C)$ and the Abel--Jacobi map
204	(Brill--Noether--Riemann--Roch), or equivalently as the identity component of the
205	Picard scheme. Both routes require infrastructure not yet available in Mathlib
206	(quotients of schemes by finite group actions; FGA representability), so the

208	theorem nonempty_jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
209	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
210	    Nonempty (JacobianWitness C) :=

217	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
218	    JacobianWitness C :=
219	  Classical.choice (nonempty_jacobianWitness C)

222	/-- The Jacobian of a smooth, proper curve over a field `k`.
223	
224	The Jacobian is the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness for `C` (see
225	`JacobianWitness` and `nonempty_jacobianWitness`); the existence of such a
226	witness is the single remaining mathematical sorry of the Phase-C scaffolding.
227	The genus-`0` specialisation is implicit in the witness — a smooth proper
228	geometrically irreducible group scheme over `k` of relative dimension `0` is
229	`Spec k` — and the genus-`0` rigidity content is absorbed into
230	`nonempty_jacobianWitness`. -/
231	noncomputable def Jacobian (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
232	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
233	  (jacobianWitness C).J
234	
235	namespace Jacobian
236	
237	/-! ## The Jacobian of `C` is an abelian variety. -/
238	
239	-- data
240	/-- The group scheme structure on the Jacobian of the curve `C`. -/
241	noncomputable instance instGrpObj : GrpObj (Jacobian C) := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
242	
243	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is smooth of relative dimension `g` over `k`, where `g` is the
244	genus of `C`. -/
245	instance smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (Jacobian C).hom :=
246	  (jacobianWitness C).smoothGenus
247	
248	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is proper over `k`. -/
249	instance instIsProper : IsProper (Jacobian C).hom := (jacobianWitness C).proper
250	
251	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
252	instance instGeometricallyIrreducible : GeometricallyIrreducible (Jacobian C).hom :=
253	  (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
254	
255	end Jacobian
256	
257	end AlgebraicGeometry

10	
11	The keystone classical input for the M2.a sub-step of the genus-0
12	Albanese-witness argument (Jacobian.tex § C.2): morphisms from the
13	projective line `ℙ¹_{k̄}` to a smooth proper geometrically irreducible
14	group scheme `A_{k̄}` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` that hit
15	the identity at a `k̄`-rational point are the constant morphism at the
16	identity. Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, Chapter~II~§4.

18	See `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex`.

23	`sorry` body. The body closure is gated on the shared cotangent-
24	vanishing Mathlib pile (`analogies/cotangent-vanishing-pile.md`,

26	into sub-steps C.2.b (reduction to `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen`),
27	C.2.c (image-dimension dichotomy), C.2.d (the cotangent-vanishing
28	keystone), and C.2.e (set-to-scheme promotion) is documented in

6	import Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic
7	import Mathlib.RingTheory.Unramified.Basic
8	import Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Basic
9	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme
10	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.FinitePresentation
11	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth
12	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Presheaf
13	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Presheaf
14	
15	/-! # Relative Kähler differentials for schemes (presheaf form)
16	
17	This file constructs the **presheaf** of relative Kähler differentials
18	`Ω_{X/S}` of a morphism of schemes `f : X → S` and states the
19	characterisation of smoothness in terms of pointwise local freeness of
20	`Ω` on affine opens.
21	
22	The construction builds on Mathlib's ring-theoretic `KaehlerDifferential`
23	and the presheaf-of-modules differential construction
24	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`.
25	
26	## References
27	
28	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
38	
39	variable {X S : Scheme.{u}}
40	
41	/-- The relative cotangent **presheaf** of a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ S`.
42	
43	On each open `U ⊆ X`, the sections are the Kähler differential module of
44	the ring map `O_S(f(U)) → O_X(U)` induced by `f`. More precisely, we use
45	the inverse-image presheaf `f⁻¹ O_S` on `X` and the canonical map to `O_X`;
46	the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.
47	
48	Mathlib leverage: `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` for `f⁻¹`,
49	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
50	for the presheaf of Kähler differentials. -/
51	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
52	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
53	    f.c
54	  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'
55	
56	/-- Localisation-compatibility identity: on each open `V ⊆ X`, the sections
57	of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f` over `V` are *definitionally* the
58	Kähler differential module of the ring map
59	`(f⁻¹ O_S)(V) → O_X(V)`. -/
60	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler (f : X ⟶ S)
61	    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
62	    ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj V : Type _) =
63	      CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential
64	        (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat
65	          f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c |>.app V) :=
66	  rfl
67	
68	/-- The Kähler module of a localization is subsingleton.
69	This is project-local Lemma `lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton`. -/
70	theorem kaehler_localization_subsingleton
71	    {A L : Type u} [CommRing A] [CommRing L] [Algebra A L]
72	    (M : Submonoid A) [IsLocalization M L] :
73	    Subsingleton (Ω[L⁄A]) :=
74	  letI : Algebra.FormallyUnramified A L :=
75	    Algebra.FormallyUnramified.of_isLocalization (R := A) (Rₘ := L) M
76	  Algebra.FormallyUnramified.subsingleton_kaehlerDifferential
77	
78	/-- Tower-cancellation `LinearEquiv` for the Kähler module under a localization:
79	if `A → L` is a localization at `M ⊆ A` and `L → B` is a ring map forming a
80	scalar tower `A → L → B`, then the canonical map `Ω[B/A] → Ω[B/L]` is a
81	`B`-linear equivalence.
82	
83	This is project-local Lemma `lem:kaehler_quotient_localization_iso` and the most
84	extractable Mathlib contribution candidate of milestone M1 (candidate name
85	`KaehlerDifferential.equivOfFormallyUnramified`). -/
86	noncomputable def kaehler_quotient_localization_iso
87	    {A L B : Type u} [CommRing A] [CommRing L] [CommRing B]
88	    [Algebra A L] [Algebra A B] [Algebra L B] [IsScalarTower A L B]
89	    (M : Submonoid A) [IsLocalization M L] :
90	    Ω[B⁄A] ≃ₗ[B] Ω[B⁄L] := by
91	  haveI : Subsingleton (Ω[L⁄A]) := kaehler_localization_subsingleton M
92	  refine LinearEquiv.ofBijective (KaehlerDifferential.map A L B B) ⟨?_, ?_⟩
93	  · rw [injective_iff_map_eq_zero]
94	    intro x hx
95	    obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ :=
96	      (KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map A L B x).mp hx
97	    -- y : B ⊗[L] Ω[L⁄A]; since Ω[L⁄A] is subsingleton, the tensor product is too
98	    haveI : Subsingleton (TensorProduct L B (Ω[L⁄A])) := by
99	      refine ⟨fun a b => ?_⟩
100	      have hzero : ∀ z : TensorProduct L B (Ω[L⁄A]), z = 0 := by
101	        intro z
102	        induction z with
103	        | zero => rfl
104	        | tmul x y =>
105	          rw [show y = (0 : Ω[L⁄A]) from Subsingleton.elim _ _, TensorProduct.tmul_zero]
106	        | add x y hx hy => rw [hx, hy, add_zero]
107	      rw [hzero a, hzero b]
108	    rw [Subsingleton.elim y 0, map_zero]
109	  · exact KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective _ _ _
110	
111	/-- Forward direction of the Jacobian criterion (algebra-Kähler form).
112	If `f : X → S` is smooth of relative dimension `n`, then for every
113	point `x ∈ X` there exist affine opens `U ⊆ S` and `V ⊆ X` with
114	`f V ⊆ U` and `x ∈ V`, on which the Kähler differential module
115	`Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]` (over the appLE algebra structure) is a free
116	module of rank `n` over `Γ(X, V)`.
117	
118	The reverse direction (locally free Ω of rank `n` implies smooth of
119	relative dimension `n`) is **mathematically false** as stated without
120	additional deformation-theoretic input (vanishing of
121	`Algebra.H1Cotangent A B` on each chart); see the chapter
122	`Differentials.tex` for the counterexample (`Spec k → Spec k[t]`,
123	`t ↦ 0`) and the converse-direction disclosure. -/
124	theorem smooth_locally_free_omega {n : ℕ} (f : X ⟶ S)
125	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n f] :
126	    ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : S.Opens) (V : X.Opens) (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
127	        x ∈ V ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧ IsAffineOpen V ∧
128	          letI : Algebra Γ(S, U) Γ(X, V) :=
129	            (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e).hom.toAlgebra
130	          (Module.Free Γ(X, V) (Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]) ∧
131	           Module.rank Γ(X, V) (Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]) = n) := by
132	  intro x
133	  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hxV, e, hRing⟩ :=
134	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension.exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension (n := n) (f := f) x
135	  refine ⟨U, V, e, hxV, hU, hV, ?_, ?_⟩ <;>
136	    · algebraize [CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e)]
137	      haveI : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth Γ(S, U) Γ(X, V) :=
138	        Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth n
139	      haveI : Nonempty V := ⟨⟨x, hxV⟩⟩
140	      first
141	      | exact Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential
142	      | exact Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential n
143	
144	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian
7	
8	/-!
9	# The Abel-Jacobi map
10	
11	The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian, and the universal property
12	of the Jacobian as the Albanese variety.
13	
14	## Status (iteration 073 — Phase E closes by reduction)
15	
16	After the iter-073 refactor of `Jacobian C := (jacobianWitness C).J` (no genus-0 dite),
17	all three protected declarations reduce uniformly to the Albanese universal property
18	carried by `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P : IsAlbanese C P (jacobianWitness C).J`:
19	
20	* `ofCurve P := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve`.
21	* `comp_ofCurve P := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).comp_ofCurve`.
22	* `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp P f hf := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf`.
23	
24	The genus-0 rigidity content previously sitting at the existence step of
25	`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` (classical rigidity `Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)`) is now absorbed
26	into the single deferred existence claim `nonempty_jacobianWitness` in
27	`Jacobian.lean`: the witness's `isAlbaneseFor P` field must verify the Albanese property
28	for genus-0 curves too, which is precisely the rigidity content.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
40	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
41	  [IsProper C.hom]
42	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
43	
44	namespace Jacobian
45	
46	variable {C} in
47	-- data
48	/-- The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian associated
49	to a `k`-rational point of `C`. Projects the Albanese universal morphism carried
50	by the witness `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. -/
51	noncomputable def ofCurve (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) : C ⟶ Jacobian C := by
52	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
53	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
54	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
55	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
56	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve
57	
58	variable {C} in
59	/-- The Abel-Jacobi map sends the `k`-rational point `P` to `0`, where `0` (denoted by `η` below) is
60	the neutral element of the group scheme `Jacobian C`. Projects the pointed-property
61	of the Albanese witness `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. -/
62	lemma comp_ofCurve (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) :
63	    P ≫ ofCurve P = η[Jacobian C] := by
64	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
65	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
66	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
67	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
68	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).comp_ofCurve
69	
70	variable {C} in
71	/--
72	The universal property of the Jacobian variety: For any abelian variety `A`,
73	any morphism `f : C ⟶ A` such that `f(P) = 0` factors uniquely through the
74	Jacobian of `C`.
75	In other words, `Jacobian C` is the Albanese variety of `C`.
76	
77	The existence-and-uniqueness data is projected unconditionally from the witness
78	`(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. The genus-0 rigidity content (`Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)`,
79	Mumford §4) is absorbed into the deferred existence claim `nonempty_jacobianWitness`,
80	since the witness must verify the Albanese property for genus-0 curves too.
81	-/
82	theorem exists_unique_ofCurve_comp (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
83	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [Smooth A.hom] [IsProper A.hom] [GrpObj A]
84	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
85	    ∃! (g : Jacobian C ⟶ A), f = ofCurve P ≫ g := by
86	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
87	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
88	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
89	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
90	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf
91	
92	end Jacobian
93	
94	end AlgebraicGeometry

9	/-!
10	# Mayer-Vietoris LES core for `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology
11	
12	This file (iter-063 split, cohort 1) contains the MV LES core for
13	`Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`-valued cohomology: the Mathlib gap-fill
14	`Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv` plus the iter-016 → iter-026 abstract
15	Mayer-Vietoris infrastructure (`HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor`,
16	`HModule'_toBiprod`, `HModule'_fromBiprod`, `HModule'_δ`, `HModule'_sequence`,
17	`HModule'_sequence_exact`, and the `δ`-zero simp companions).
18	
19	This is cohort 1 of the original `MayerVietoris.lean` split:
20	* `MayerVietorisCore.lean` (this file): MV LES core + Mathlib gap-fill.
21	* `MayerVietorisCover.lean`: 2-affine cover MV + cover-totality bridges +
22	  `IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers + `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` carrier.
23	
24	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex`.
25	-/
26	
27	set_option autoImplicit false
28	
29	universe u v w w'
30	
31	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
32	
33	/-! ## Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill: `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` as a `LinearEquiv`
34	
35	Mathlib's `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv : Ext.{w} X Y n ≃ Ext.{w'} X Y n`
36	(`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean` L540) is a bare
37	`Equiv` only. The iter-034 universe-bridge `HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv`
38	(below in `section CoverTotality`) requires it as a `LinearEquiv` over the
39	`Linear R C`-enriched abelian category. The upgrade is mechanical: the
40	`AddCommGroup (Ext X Y n)` and `Module R (Ext X Y n)` instances are themselves
41	transferred from the standard derived category through `homEquiv`, and
42	Mathlib's load-bearing intermediate lemma `Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv` (Basic.lean
43	L543–545) shows that `chgUniv` preserves the underlying derived-category
44	morphism. So `chgUniv` automatically commutes with `+` and `•`. Two short
45	helper lemmas (`chgUniv_add` and `chgUniv_smul`) plus a 7-line `LinearEquiv`
46	structure literal give the upgrade.
47	
48	This cohort lives outside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme` because the
49	helpers belong to the `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext` namespace. We use the
50	qualified-name pattern `Abelian.Ext.foo` rather than wrapping the entire file
51	in a second namespace block. -/
52	
53	/-- Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill (a): `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` is additive. The
54	underlying `homEquiv` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean`
55	L162) is preserved by `chgUniv` (Mathlib lemma `Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv`,
56	L543–545), and the `AddCommGroup (Ext X Y n)` instance (L241–243) is defined by
57	transferring the `AddCommGroup` from the standard derived category through
58	`homEquiv`. Combining these via the `Ext.ext` extensionality (L177–179) gives
59	additivity. -/
60	private lemma Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_add
61	    {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C]
62	    [HasExt.{w} C] [HasExt.{w'} C] {X Y : C} {n : ℕ} (a b : Abelian.Ext.{w} X Y n) :
63	    Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (a + b) =
64	      Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a + Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} b := by
65	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
66	  ext
67	  rw [Abelian.Ext.add_hom, ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply,
68	      ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply, ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply]
69	  change Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (a + b)) =
70	    Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a) +
71	      Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} b)
72	  rw [Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv, Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv,
73	      Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv, ← Abelian.Ext.add_hom]
74	
75	/-- Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill (b): `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` is `R`-linear when `C`
76	is `Linear R`-enriched. Same proof shape as `chgUniv_add`, but using
77	`smul_hom` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Linear.lean` L52–55)
78	in place of `add_hom`. -/
79	private lemma Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_smul

407	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
408	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
409	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
410	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
411	    (HModule'_shortComplex k S).ShortExact where
412	  exact := HModule'_shortComplex_exact k S
413	
414	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-021): the connecting homomorphism `δ` of the
415	Mayer-Vietoris long exact sequence in `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology.
416	For a Mayer-Vietoris square `S` and adjacent cohomological degrees `n₀ + 1 = n₁`,
417	the morphism
418	  δ : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₀).obj (op S.X₁) ⟶
419	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₁).obj (op S.X₄)
420	in `AddCommGrpCat` is the precomposition with the extension class
421	`(HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact k S).extClass : Ext (...X₁...) (...X₃...) 1`
422	followed by `AddCommGrpCat.ofHom` to wrap the resulting `AddMonoidHom` as a
423	categorical morphism. Direct mirror of Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.δ`
424	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` L112–114)
425	for the `ModuleCat k` flavor with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
426	
427	The `[HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]` typeclass is required (the
428	`Ext.precomp` operation depends on it) and matches the corresponding
429	requirement on iter-016 `HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor` / `..._cohomologyPresheaf`,
430	iter-017 `HModule'_toBiprod` / `..._fromBiprod`, and iter-018
431	`HModule'_toBiprod_fromBiprod`.
432	
433	This connecting hom is the missing third link of the Mayer-Vietoris exact
434	sequence: combined with iter-017's `toBiprod` (sum of restriction) and
435	`fromBiprod` (difference of restriction), iter-018's `toBiprod ≫ fromBiprod = 0`,
436	and the iter-022+ packaging into a `ComposableArrows`-form sequence + iter-023+
437	sequence-iso to `Ext.contravariantSequence` + iter-024+ exactness theorem, it
438	will yield the full LES `... → Hⁿ(X₄) → Hⁿ(X₂) ⊞ Hⁿ(X₃) → Hⁿ(X₁) → Hⁿ⁺¹(X₄) → ...`
439	in `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology. -/
440	noncomputable def HModule'_δ
441	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
442	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
443	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
444	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
445	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
446	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
447	    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₀).obj (Opposite.op S.X₁) ⟶
448	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₁).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄) :=
449	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact k S).extClass.precomp _ (by omega))
450	
451	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-022): the Mayer-Vietoris long exact sequence
452	in `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology, packaged as a `ComposableArrows` of
453	length 5 in `AddCommGrpCat`. The five composable morphisms are, in order,
454	`HModule'_toBiprod` at degree `n₀`, `HModule'_fromBiprod` at degree `n₀`, the
455	connecting hom `HModule'_δ` from degree `n₀` to degree `n₁`, `HModule'_toBiprod`
456	at degree `n₁`, and `HModule'_fromBiprod` at degree `n₁`. Direct mirror of
457	Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.sequence`
458	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` L120–122)
459	for the `ModuleCat k` flavor.
460	
461	The `noncomputable abbrev` form is load-bearing: downstream `dsimp`-based
462	unfolding (in iter-023+ `HModule'_sequenceIso` and iter-024+
463	`HModule'_sequence_exact`) needs to access `mk₅`'s field-projection simp
464	lemmas through the abbrev. -/
465	noncomputable abbrev HModule'_sequence
466	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
467	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
468	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
469	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
470	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
471	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
472	    ComposableArrows AddCommGrpCat 5 :=
473	  ComposableArrows.mk₅ (HModule'_toBiprod k S F n₀) (HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n₀)
474	    (HModule'_δ k S F n₀ n₁ h)
475	    (HModule'_toBiprod k S F n₁) (HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n₁)
476	
477	/-- Iter-023 aux lemma 1: explicit elementwise formula for `HModule'_toBiprod`
478	(mirror Mathlib `MayerVietoris.lean` L48–64). -/
479	lemma HModule'_toBiprod_apply
480	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
481	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
482	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
483	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
484	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
485	    {n : ℕ} (y : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄)) :
486	    HModule'_toBiprod k S F n y =
487	      (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).inv
488	        ⟨(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₂₄.op y,
489	          (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₃₄.op y⟩ := by
490	  apply (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv.injective
491	  dsimp [HModule'_toBiprod]
492	  ext
493	  · rw [Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply, Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply,
494	      ← AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_fst_apply, Iso.hom_inv_id_apply,
495	      ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, biprod.lift_fst, Iso.inv_hom_id_apply]
496	  · rw [Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply, Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply,
497	      ← AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_snd_apply, Iso.hom_inv_id_apply,
498	      ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, biprod.lift_snd, Iso.inv_hom_id_apply]
499	
500	/-- Iter-023 aux lemma 2: explicit elementwise formula for `HModule'_fromBiprod`
501	on the inverse of `biprodIsoProd` (mirror Mathlib L77–83). -/
502	lemma HModule'_fromBiprod_biprodIsoProd_inv_apply
503	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
504	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
505	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
506	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
507	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
508	    {n : ℕ}
509	    (y₁ : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂))
510	    (y₂ : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃)) :
511	    HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n
512	        ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).inv ⟨y₁, y₂⟩) =
513	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₂.op y₁ -
514	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op y₂ := by
515	  dsimp [HModule'_fromBiprod]
516	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
517	  simp [AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_desc, sub_eq_add_neg]
518	
519	-- Iter-023 aux lemma 3: bridges `AddCommGrpCat`-side `biprodIsoProd` and
520	-- `Ext`-side `Ext.biprodAddEquiv` for the `toBiprod` morphism
521	-- (mirror Mathlib L85–91). The `set_option ... in` and `attribute ... in`
522	-- wrappers match Mathlib L85, L86 verbatim.
523	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
524	attribute [local simp] HModule'_toBiprod_apply in
525	lemma HModule'_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom_toBiprod_apply
526	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
527	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
528	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
529	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
530	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
531	    {n : ℕ} (x : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄)) :
532	    Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm
533	        ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).hom (HModule'_toBiprod k S F n x)) =
534	      (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (HModule'_shortComplex k S).g).comp x (zero_add n) :=
535	  Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.injective (by cat_disch)
536	
537	-- Iter-023 aux lemma 4: bridges the same machinery for the `fromBiprod`
538	-- morphism (mirror Mathlib L93–106).
539	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
540	attribute [local simp] sub_eq_add_neg in
541	lemma HModule'_mk₀_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom
542	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
543	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
544	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
545	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
546	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
547	    {n : ℕ}
548	    (x : ↑((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
549	           (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃))) :
550	    (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (HModule'_shortComplex k S).f).comp
551	        (Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm
552	          ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).hom x)) (zero_add n) =
553	      (HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n x) := by
554	  obtain ⟨⟨x₂, x₃⟩, rfl⟩ :=
555	    (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv.symm.surjective x
556	  dsimp
557	  rw [Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv_symm_apply,
558	    Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_symm_apply,
559	    HModule'_fromBiprod_biprodIsoProd_inv_apply]
560	  cat_disch
561	
562	-- Iter-023 main: comparison iso from the iter-022 LES sequence to
563	-- `Ext.contravariantSequence` (mirror Mathlib L124–138). The technical heart
564	-- of the Mayer-Vietoris LES.
565	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
566	noncomputable def HModule'_sequenceIso
567	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
568	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
569	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
570	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
571	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
572	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
573	    HModule'_sequence k S F n₀ n₁ h ≅
574	      Abelian.Ext.contravariantSequence (HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact k S)
575	        F n₀ n₁ (by omega) :=
576	  ComposableArrows.isoMk₅ (Iso.refl _)
577	    ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).trans
578	      (Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm).toAddCommGrpIso)
579	    (Iso.refl _) (Iso.refl _)
580	    ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).trans
581	      (Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm).toAddCommGrpIso)
582	    (Iso.refl _)
583	    (by ext; apply HModule'_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom_toBiprod_apply)
584	    (by ext; symm; apply HModule'_mk₀_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom)
585	    (by dsimp; rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]; rfl)
586	    (by ext; apply HModule'_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom_toBiprod_apply)
587	    (by ext; symm; apply HModule'_mk₀_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom)
588	
589	/-- Iter-026: Mayer-Vietoris LES exactness theorem (mirror Mathlib
590	`MayerVietoris.lean` L140–141). The iter-022 sequence is exact via the iter-023
591	comparison iso to `Ext.contravariantSequence`. -/
592	lemma HModule'_sequence_exact
593	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
594	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
595	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
596	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
597	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
598	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
599	    (HModule'_sequence k S F n₀ n₁ h).Exact :=
600	  ComposableArrows.exact_of_iso (HModule'_sequenceIso k S F n₀ n₁ h).symm
601	    (Abelian.Ext.contravariantSequence_exact _ _ _ _ _)
602	
603	/-- Iter-026: `δ ≫ toBiprod = 0` simp companion (mirror Mathlib L143–145). -/
604	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
605	lemma HModule'_δ_toBiprod
606	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
607	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
608	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
609	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
610	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
611	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
612	    HModule'_δ k S F n₀ n₁ h ≫ HModule'_toBiprod k S F n₁ = 0 :=
613	  (HModule'_sequence_exact k S F n₀ n₁ h).zero 2
614	
615	/-- Iter-026: `fromBiprod ≫ δ = 0` simp companion (mirror Mathlib L147–149). -/
616	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
617	lemma HModule'_fromBiprod_δ
618	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
619	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
620	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
621	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
622	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
623	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
624	    HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n₀ ≫ HModule'_δ k S F n₀ n₁ h = 0 :=
625	  (HModule'_sequence_exact k S F n₀ n₁ h).zero 1
626	
627	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

8	/-!
9	# Mayer-Vietoris on a 2-affine cover and Čech-acyclic cover infrastructure
10	
11	This file (iter-063 split, cohort 2) contains the 2-affine cover
12	Mayer-Vietoris theorems, the cover-totality bridges (universe-shift
13	`LinearEquiv`s, `finrank` / `Module.Finite` transports), the
14	`IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers, the comparison-iso typeclass
15	`HasCechToHModuleIso`, and the existence-bundled
16	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` predicate together with the producer that
17	fires `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` from a `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover`
18	hypothesis.
19	
20	This is cohort 2 of the original `MayerVietoris.lean` split:
21	* `MayerVietorisCore.lean`: MV LES core + Mathlib gap-fill.
22	* `MayerVietorisCover.lean` (this file): 2-affine cover MV +
23	  cover-totality bridges + `IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers +
24	  `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` carrier.
25	
26	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex`.
27	-/
28	
29	set_option autoImplicit false
30	
31	universe u v w w'
32	
33	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
36	
37	section AffineCoverMVSquare
38	
39	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-028): a bundled 2-affine cover of a scheme
40	together with the affineness of the pairwise intersection. The geometric input
41	to the abstract Mayer-Vietoris LES `HModule'_sequence_exact` consumes — packaged
42	to record the affineness conditions and the cover-totality hypothesis that the
43	abstract MV-square does not capture. The accessor `toMayerVietorisSquare`
44	produces the underlying Mathlib `MayerVietorisSquare`.
45	
46	Mathematically: a 2-affine open cover of `X` whose pairwise intersection is also
47	affine. The cover hypothesis `cover : U₁ ⊔ U₂ = ⊤` is load-bearing for downstream
48	Serre-finiteness use (it identifies the `MayerVietorisSquare` corner `X₄` with
49	the whole scheme `X`). -/
50	structure AffineCoverMVSquare (X : Scheme.{u}) where
51	  /-- First affine open of the cover. -/
52	  U₁ : X.Opens
53	  /-- Second affine open of the cover. -/
54	  U₂ : X.Opens
55	  /-- `U₁` is affine. -/
56	  isAffineOpen_U₁ : IsAffineOpen U₁
57	  /-- `U₂` is affine. -/
58	  isAffineOpen_U₂ : IsAffineOpen U₂
59	  /-- The intersection `U₁ ⊓ U₂` is affine. -/
60	  isAffineOpen_inf : IsAffineOpen (U₁ ⊓ U₂)
61	  /-- Total cover: `U₁ ⊔ U₂ = ⊤`. -/
62	  cover : U₁ ⊔ U₂ = ⊤
63	
64	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-028): the underlying abstract Mayer-Vietoris
65	square produced by a 2-affine cover. Bridge from the geometric input (an
66	`AffineCoverMVSquare`) to the categorical input (a `MayerVietorisSquare` for
67	the Grothendieck topology on `Opens X.toTopCat`) the abstract MV-LES theorem
68	`HModule'_sequence_exact` consumes. The four corners collapse definitionally:
69	`X₁ = U₁ ⊓ U₂`, `X₂ = U₁`, `X₃ = U₂`, `X₄ = U₁ ⊔ U₂` (each by `rfl`,
70	plan-agent probe-verified). -/
71	noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare {X : Scheme.{u}}
72	    (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
73	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat).MayerVietorisSquare :=
74	  Opens.mayerVietorisSquare S.U₁ S.U₂
75	
76	/-- Iter-029 corner identification: `X₁ = U₁ ⊓ U₂`. The first three corners are
77	`rfl`-equal because `Opens.mayerVietorisSquare` defines them definitionally. -/
78	@[simp]
79	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₁
80	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
81	    S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₁ = S.U₁ ⊓ S.U₂ := rfl
82	
83	/-- Iter-029 corner identification: `X₂ = U₁`. -/
84	@[simp]
85	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₂
86	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
87	    S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₂ = S.U₁ := rfl
88	
89	/-- Iter-029 corner identification: `X₃ = U₂`. -/
90	@[simp]
91	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₃
92	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
93	    S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₃ = S.U₂ := rfl
94	
95	/-- Iter-029 cover-totality identification: `X₄ = ⊤`. The substantive corner
96	identification — uses the `cover` field, which is the totality hypothesis
97	`U₁ ⊔ U₂ = ⊤` recorded in `AffineCoverMVSquare`. -/
98	@[simp]
99	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₄
100	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
101	    S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₄ = ⊤ := S.cover
102	
103	/-- Iter-029 Mayer-Vietoris LES sequence on a 2-affine cover: a routine
104	specialisation of the iter-022 abstract sequence `HModule'_sequence` to the
105	bundled `AffineCoverMVSquare` data. Parameterised over a generic sheaf `F`
106	for reusability; the `toModuleKSheaf C` application is iter-030+ work. The
107	fully-qualified `_root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_sequence` reference
108	is required because `def AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence` auto-opens the
109	`AffineCoverMVSquare` namespace inside the body, causing bare
110	`HModule'_sequence` to recursively resolve to itself. -/
111	noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence
112	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare)
113	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
114	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
115	    ComposableArrows AddCommGrpCat 5 :=
116	  _root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_sequence k S.toMayerVietorisSquare F n₀ n₁ h
117	
118	/-- Iter-029 Mayer-Vietoris LES exactness on a 2-affine cover: a routine
119	specialisation of the iter-026 abstract exactness theorem
120	`HModule'_sequence_exact` to the bundled `AffineCoverMVSquare` data. -/
121	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence_exact
122	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare)
123	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
124	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
125	    (S.HModule'_sequence k F n₀ n₁ h).Exact :=
126	  _root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_sequence_exact k S.toMayerVietorisSquare F n₀ n₁ h
127	
128	/-- Iter-030 Mayer-Vietoris LES on a 2-affine cover of a curve: a routine

155	/-- Iter-031 cover-totality source-object identification. For a category `C`
156	with terminal object `T`, the sheafification of the free `k`-module on the
157	representable presheaf at `T` is canonically isomorphic to the constant sheaf
158	at `ModuleCat.of k k`. This is the natural-iso prerequisite for identifying
159	`HModule' k F n T` (open-evaluation cohomology at the terminal `T`, iter-014)
160	with `HModule k F n` (global cohomology, iter-009): both are `Ext` groups
161	whose source objects are made canonically isomorphic by this iso.
162	
163	Assembled from three Mathlib pieces, mirroring the structure
164	`constantSheaf J D = Functor.const Cᵒᵖ ⋙ presheafToSheaf J D`:
165	1. `yoneda.obj T ≅ (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj PUnit` — the terminal-collapse
166	   of the representable, by `IsTerminal.from`/`IsTerminal.hom_ext`.
167	2. `Functor.constComp _ PUnit (ModuleCat.free k)` — the identity
168	   `(Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj PUnit ⋙ ModuleCat.free k =
169	    (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj ((ModuleCat.free k).obj PUnit)`
170	   packaged as an iso.
171	3. `(Finsupp.LinearEquiv.finsuppUnique k k PUnit).toModuleIso` —
172	   `(ModuleCat.free k).obj PUnit ≅ ModuleCat.of k k`. -/
173	noncomputable def HModule'_top_sourceIso
174	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
175	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
176	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
177	    {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
178	    (presheafToSheaf J _).obj
179	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj T)
180	      ≅ (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) :=
181	  (presheafToSheaf J _).mapIso
182	    (Functor.isoWhiskerRight
183	        (NatIso.ofComponents
184	          (fun _ => Equiv.toIso { toFun := fun _ => PUnit.unit
185	                                  invFun := fun _ => hT.from _
186	                                  left_inv := fun _ => hT.hom_ext _ _
187	                                  right_inv := fun _ => rfl })
188	          (fun _ => by ext; rfl))
189	        (ModuleCat.free k) ≪≫
190	      Functor.constComp _ PUnit.{u+1} (ModuleCat.free k) ≪≫
191	      (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).mapIso
192	        (Finsupp.LinearEquiv.finsuppUnique k k PUnit).toModuleIso)

194	/-- Iter-032 cover-totality Ext-transport at universe `u+1`. Combining the

196	objects of two `Ext` computations) with the Mathlib Ext-API operation
197	`precompOfLinear` (pre-composition of `Ext` along an `Ext 0`-element) yields
198	a `k`-linear equivalence between the universe-`u+1` Ext at the sheafified
199	representable on `T` and `HModule k F n`.
